item.title: LETTER: Stop parking like it’s valet at St. Paris Shoppers
item.description: Two-minute errands on W. Main have become a municipal crisis of character.

item.title: Mechanicsburg reunion Facebook event toxic six months early
item.description: The class of whenever has already split into cliques again in the Burg chat.

item.title: Congress Votes to Investigate the Investigation of the Investigation
item.description: Lawmakers authorized another probe into prior probes, ensuring full employment for lawyers and partial employment for truth. What do you think?

item.title: Dad buys Indian Lake boat the family did not ask for, will use twice
item.description: The trailer is already a personality in a Mechanicsburg driveway.

item.title: Woman corrects out-of-towner asking for directions near Monument Square
item.description: There is a wrong way to say local places. There is always a wrong way.

item.title: Congress Debates Whether AI Can Serve as a Committee Witness
item.description: Lawmakers are weighing a proposal that would allow artificial intelligence systems to deliver sworn testimony before congressional committees, a move supporters call efficiency and critics call a cry …

item.title: Man brags about not needing GPS on US-36, arrives 40 minutes late
item.description: “I know a back way between the Burg and Urbana” has claimed another evening.

item.title: St. Paris committee formed to plan another committee for fall festival
item.description: The festival is in October. The email chain on Springfield Street is already infinite.

item.title: Scientists Announce First Lab-Grown Blood Available in Hospitals
item.description: Researchers say synthetic blood could ease shortages nationwide after successful early clinical use, though patients still prefer the kind that comes with a story about a high school football injury. …

item.title: Man, 41, still introduces himself by what he played for the Indians
item.description: “I played ball at the Burg” remains his LinkedIn before LinkedIn.

item.title: Neighbor war erupts over leaf-blower hours on Sandusky Street
item.description: 7:58 a.m. has been declared either “morning” or “violence,” depending on the driveway.

item.title: Major Social Network Tests 'Pay to Be Seen by Your Own Friends' Feature
item.description: A leading social platform is piloting fees that prioritize posts to friends and family, effectively charging users for the attention they used to get for free by being annoying. What do you think?

item.title: Uncle explains national politics using only US-36 and US-68 metaphors
item.description: The country is apparently stuck in the left lane around Monument Square going 22.

item.title: Woman frames Urbana rec softball plaque like it's a Super Bowl ring
item.description: Third place in a coed league has never looked more official on Market Street energy.

item.title: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady, Markets React by Doing Nothing Helpful
item.description: The Federal Reserve left interest rates unchanged, prompting analysts to issue 14 different explanations and homeowners to stare at their mortgages like they might blink first. What do you think?

item.title: Man furious Amazon driver used his gravel driveway as intended
item.description: Tracks in the stone have been logged like a crime scene off a county road near Mutual.

item.title: Triad boosters nearly dissolve over exact shade of Cardinal red
item.description: “True red” versus “too pink in the sun” has divided the Brush Lake Road chat.

item.title: NASA Confirms Humans Will Return to the Moon 'Sometime Before Everyone Involved Retires'
item.description: Space agency officials updated lunar timelines again, assuring the public that boots will touch the Moon in a window wide enough to include several presidential administrations and at least one new ph…

item.title: Mechanicsburg zoning talk about a 4-foot fence lasts three hours
item.description: Democracy thrived at the village offices on N. Main. Nobody thrived.

item.title: Teen gets job at Frosty’s; parents act like he joined the Marines
item.description: Scooping pizza energy on N. Main has been rebranded as “character.”

item.title: Supreme Court Agrees to Hear Case About Whether Phones Count as 'Being Present'
item.description: Justices will consider a dispute over digital presence and legal obligations, a question that already ruins every dinner table in America without needing a ruling. What do you think?

item.title: Couple extremely loud about how quiet their Indian Lake weekend was
item.description: Nobody in Mechanicsburg asked. The photo dump arrived anyway.

item.title: Township man treats recycling rules as more of a vibe
item.description: Pizza boxes with cheese still on them have been described as “basically cardboard” near Cable.

item.title: Automakers Race to Add AI Dashboards That Argue Back About Your Driving
item.description: New vehicle software will critique acceleration, following distance, and 'vibes,' turning every commute into a performance review you cannot transfer to another department. What do you think?

item.title: Man shares same weather take seven times between Main and Sandusky
item.description: “Hot one today” remains undefeated as a social strategy in the Burg.

item.title: Woman keeps mental scoreboard of who brings store-bought cookies to Graham events
item.description: The list is not written down. The list is eternal. The campus is on US-36.

item.title: World Health Officials Urge Calm After New Virus Variant With a Confusing Name
item.description: Health agencies say a newly tracked variant is worth watching but not panicking over, advice the public will ignore in opposite directions simultaneously. What do you think?

item.title: Guy at hardware counter will not stop talking about his zero-turn
item.description: A man buying weed killer on a Urbana errand accidentally entered a 40-minute masterclass.

item.title: Parents furious Indians youth baseball game started on time
item.description: A 6:00 first pitch at 6:00 in Mechanicsburg has been described as “aggressive.”

item.title: Congress Proposes TikTok Ban Except for Everyone Who Already Has It
item.description: Legislators advanced another round of restrictions on foreign-owned social apps while carefully protecting the exact behavior they claim to fear. What do you think?

item.title: Church potluck sign-up sheet causes low-grade cold war
item.description: Three people signed up for desserts. Zero for green beans. Peace talks ongoing in North Lewisburg.

item.title: Man pulls over on US-36 to correct stranger about corn height
item.description: Agronomy was not requested between Mechanicsburg and Urbana. Agronomy was delivered.

item.title: Major Bank Outage Leaves Millions Unable to Venmo Their Share of the Appetizer
item.description: A widespread banking app failure disrupted digital payments for hours, forcing Americans to confront the ancient ritual of actually owing each other cash. What do you think?

item.title: Dad extremely proud of completely mediocre fantasy baseball record
item.description: Finishing 6–7 in a league of Graham and Mechanicsburg dads has been framed as “building culture.”

item.title: Village debates whether flashing speed sign on Main is 'too city'
item.description: Mechanicsburg council split on whether digital numbers protect kids or invite Columbus energy.

item.title: Researchers Say Microplastics Found in Clouds, Human Brains, and Probably Your Favorite Mug
item.description: New studies detected microplastics in yet more places once considered pure, prompting experts to recommend fewer plastic bags and more existential dread. What do you think?

item.title: Local man wins argument about which Burg coffee is better
item.description: A 22-minute debate at the corner of Main and Sandusky ended the way all great debates end: with nobody changing their mind and everyone late for work.

item.title: Woman still mad about 2019 Champaign County Fair parking
item.description: A St. Paris resident would like to revisit a grassy lot decision from the first Trump term.

item.title: United Nations Hosts Climate Summit Where Leaders Arrive in Very Large Planes
item.description: World leaders gathered to discuss emissions reductions after traveling by methods that make the average pickup truck look like a bicycle with manners. What do you think?

item.title: Triad hires townie Bill Hickley as baseball coach amid community uproar
item.description: Board votes for lifelong local William “Bill” Hickley; parents petition, Cardinals kids cheer, and the parking lot off Maple Street has opinions.
